prompt:
A simple DC-blocking filter is required. It must pass signals at 7.61 kHz with less than 1.5 dB attenuation while rejecting frequencies at 70.2 Hz by at least 30.1 dB. The circuit must use only passive components and should be as low-cost as possible.

REQUIREMENTS:
  Type: high-pass filter
  Passband edge (fc): 7.61 kHz  [max loss: 1.5 dB]
  Stopband edge (fs): 70.23 Hz  [min attenuation: 30.1 dB]
ground_truth_netlist:

C1   VIN    VOUT   2.25n
R1   VOUT   0      28k

\[ A(f) = 10 \cdot \log_{10}(1 + (\frac{f_c}{f_s})^2) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) = 10 \cdot \log_{10}(1 + (\frac{7610}{70.23})^2) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) = 10 \cdot \log_{10}(1 + 1370.2^2) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) = 10 \cdot \log_{10}(1877516.04) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) \approx 60.7 \text{ dB} \]

The calculated attenuation is more than the required 30.1 dB, so a single-stage RC high-pass filter is suitable.

Next, calculate the resistor and capacitor values:
\[ f_c = 7610 \text{ Hz} \]
\[ R \cdot C = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot f_c} \]
\[ R \cdot C = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot 7610} \]

C1   VIN   VOUT   2.02n

With \( R = 10 \text{k}\Omega \):

\[ C \approx 5.812 \text{nF} \]

prompt:
Provide a high-pass filter design for a general-purpose noise-reduction application. The -3.7 dB frequency should be no higher than 80.6 Hz. At 0.772 Hz the filter must deliver >= 38.7 dB of attenuation. No active components; keep the design as simple and cheap as possible.

  Passband edge (fc): 80.65 Hz  [max loss: 3.7 dB]
  Stopband edge (fs): 0.772 Hz  [min attenuation: 38.7 dB]

iterations.cot_reasoning:
I need a 1st-order high-pass filter.

### Step 2 — COMPUTE COMPONENT VALUES

First, calculate the product \( R \cdot C \):
\[ R \cdot C = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot f_c} = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot 80.65 \text{ Hz}} \approx 1.98 \times 10^{-3} \text{ s} \]

Choose \( R = 10 \text{k}\Omega \) (within the allowed range of 1 kΩ to 100 kΩ):
\[ C = \frac{1.98 \times 10^{-3} \text{ s}}{10 \times 10^3 \text{ Ω}} = 198 \text{nF} \]

### Step 3 — WRITE THE NETLIST

Given \( f_c = 507.8 \) Hz:
\[ R \cdot C = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot 507.8} \approx 3.15 \times 10^{-6} \, \text{s} \]

Let's choose \( R = 10 \, \text{k}\Omega \):
\[ C = \frac{3.15 \times 10^{-6}}{10 \times 10^3} = 318.3 \, \text{nF} \]
